source_old_req: The acc1_dataOutput thread shall operate with a periodic dispatch protocol, priority 2, and a period of 4000 ms, with a compute execution time bounded between 400 ms and 500 ms and a deadline of 500 ms. The thread shall accept acc1_command_in as a valid input event data port of type SHM_DataType::actionData. The thread shall guarantee that the value of acc1out is determined solely by the current input acc1_command_in and the internal computations of the thread, and that the acc1out data port adheres to the SHM_DataType::accData data type constraints at all times.
natural_requirement: The acc1_dataOutput component shall define the implementation-level signal routing for acc1_dataOutput.impl. In the implementation layer, acc1_dataOutput.impl shall route data flow from spg.acc1DataOut to acc1out through C1 and control signal from acc1_command_in to spg.event_in through C2.

requirement_items.text: The acc1_dataOutput.impl implementation shall route the data flow from spg.acc1DataOut to acc1out through connection C1.
requirement_items.source_text_before_behavior_style_rewrit: The acc1_dataOutput.impl implementation shall declare connection C1 from spg.acc1DataOut to acc1out.

requirement_items.text: The acc1_dataOutput.impl implementation shall route the control signal from acc1_command_in to spg.event_in through connection C2.
requirement_items.source_text_before_behavior_style_rewrit: The acc1_dataOutput.impl implementation shall declare connection C2 from acc1_command_in to spg.event_in.

quality_policy.traceable: Every item has trace_refs and expected_check metadata tied to AADL facts.
quality_policy.principle: Every generated sentence must name an explicit property assignment or structural connection; ordinary feature declarations are not used as rebuilt requirements.

natural_requirement_before_behavior_styl: The acc1_dataOutput.impl implementation shall declare connection C1 from spg.acc1DataOut to acc1out. The acc1_dataOutput.impl implementation shall declare connection C2 from acc1_command_in to spg.event_in.

behavior_style_policy.basis: front-110 AGREE-derived requirements favor implementable behavior, constants, assumptions, guarantees, routing, and bounded properties.
behavior_style_policy.rule: Do not describe unsupported validity/legal-boundary semantics unless the AADL model exposes variables, values, or properties that can implement them.

natural_requirement_before_paragraph_rew: The acc1_dataOutput.impl implementation shall route the data flow from spg.acc1DataOut to acc1out through connection C1. The acc1_dataOutput.impl implementation shall route the control signal from acc1_command_in to spg.event_in through connection C2.

paragraph_style_policy.rule: Req.txt is written as a natural-language requirement paragraph; per-item traceability stays in requirement_items.
paragraph_style_policy.basis: Front-110 AGREE-derived requirements generally combine function, assumptions, guarantees, and implementation notes into 1-4 sentence paragraphs.
